player_notes.jalen brunson: Played through an early injury in Game 1, scoring 30 pts including 13 in Q4. Monitor injury status for Game 2, but finished strong — no indication of restrictions.
player_notes.mitchell robinson: Available for NBA Finals after hand surgery; practiced before Game 1. His return could affect KAT's rebounding volume and frontcourt minutes distribution.

game_notes.NYK vs SAS: Mitchell Robinson available after hand surgery, potentially compressing Knicks frontcourt minutes. Brunson nursing an undisclosed early-game injury — worth monitoring official Game 2 injury report.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: The season context's playoff bracket shows R1 matchups and series diaries through R1 only. The most recent playoff round documented is R1. OKC is listed as the defending champion (2024-25). No NBA Finals matchup between NYK and SAS is documented anywhere in the context.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Multiple news items confirm the 2026 NBA Finals are currently underway between the New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs, with NYK winning Game 1 on the road 105-95. This means both teams advanced through R1, R2, and conference finals — none of which are documented in the season context. The context doc has no record of this Finals matchup, the intermediate rounds, or the series score.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add the 2026 NBA Finals matchup (NYK vs SAS, NYK leads 1-0 after Game 1 105-95 road win) to the playoff bracket section. Document all intermediate rounds (R2, ECF, WCF) that led to this matchup. Update the playoff series diary section with at minimum a Game 1 entry for the Finals.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Series diary G6 entry states: 'Brunson's left ankle status is THE critical NYK variable — his third-quarter exit in the series-clinching game introduces genuine availability and minutes-restriction uncertainty.' Last documented Brunson entry covers the ATL series (R1), where he exited G6 with a left ankle injury.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: News confirms Brunson scored 30 points in NBA Finals Game 1, including 13 points in the fourth quarter to lead a comeback from a 14-point deficit. He is active and performing at a star level with no apparent ankle restriction. Additionally, the NBA is investigating an interaction between Brunson and courtside fans in Game 1.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update Brunson's status: his left ankle concern from R1 G6 has resolved — he is healthy and performing at full capacity in the NBA Finals (30 pts in G1). Remove or downgrade the ankle concern flag. Add note about NBA investigation into fan interaction in Finals G1 (monitor for any suspension risk, though current reporting indicates investigation only).

Wembanyama expressed confidence SAS will bounce back. No new injury reported.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update Wembanyama's playoff context to reflect he is in the NBA Finals (G1 played, SAS lost 95-105). Add Finals G1 diary entry. The fan court-storming incident is noted but no injury resulted — confirm no health concern from incident. Context should reflect the series is now Knicks vs Spurs with NYK leading 1-0.
